title: MinecraftFTP
description: A carefully curated enhancement modpack that combines immersive audio improvements, visual refinements, and quality-of-life features while maintaining vanilla gameplay balance.
body:
# Minecraft FTP - For The People

## What This Modpack Does:
Minecraft FTP is a lightweight enhancement modpack that improves the vanilla Minecraft experience without requiring shaders. It adds immersive audio effects, subtle visual improvements, quality-of-life features, and navigation tools while maintaining the core vanilla gameplay.

## Why You Should Download This Modpack:
- Enhances gameplay without changing vanilla mechanics
- Improves performance compared to shader-based visual enhancements
- Adds immersive audio that makes the game world feel alive
- Includes quality-of-life improvements that reduce frustration
- Provides better navigation tools for exploration
- All mods are carefully selected to work together without conflicts

## Key Features:

**Audio Enhancements:**
- Environmental sounds that change based on your surroundings
- Realistic sound physics with echoes and reverb
- Built-in voice chat for multiplayer

**Visual Improvements:**
- Subtle particle effects and animations
- Connected textures and improved animations
- Falling leaves and other ambient details
- Extended view distance options

**Quality-of-Life Features:**
- Enhanced inventory management and sorting
- Improved mouse controls for inventory
- In-game mod configuration menu
- Minimap and world map with waypoints

## Important Information Before Downloading:
- Compatible with Minecraft version 1.21.4
- Recommended minimum RAM: 4GB
- No additional configuration required - works out of the box
- For optimal voice chat experience, a microphone is recommended but not required
- All included mods are server-safe and can be used in multiplayer

This modpack was created by me and my friends to address our desire for a better Minecraft experience without the performance impact of shaders. It's designed to be accessible to everyone, hence the name "For The People."
